body.body:
Track repeated tool dispatch failures. When a pattern exceeds a threshold, surface it as a habit repair candidate.

TRIGGER: run_bash exit code 127 (command not found) where the attempted command matches a registered tool name. Also: any tool call that errors with the same error type N times within a rolling window.

COUNTER KEY: (attempted_name, dispatch_path, error_type)
THRESHOLD: configurable, default=3
WINDOW: rolling 24h

STORAGE: misfire_counts table in Postgres, or a dedicated misfire_log.jsonl in ~/.TheIgors/logs/

SURFACING: when counter > threshold, add entry to habit repair queue (or surface in audit Step 12 habit health). Do NOT auto-fix — flag for human review.

SCOPE: instrument run_bash result handler (tools/runner.py) for exit-127 detection. Instrument tool registry execute() for repeated error detection. Do NOT change habit dispatch logic or BG scoring.

FORENSIC LOGGING: log_error when threshold crossed.

TESTS: test that exit-127 increments counter; test threshold triggers surfacing; test counter resets after window.
body.title: Misfire counter: threshold-based habit repair surfacing
body.result: MisfireCounter implementation complete: tracks repeated tool failures (exit-127 bash, repeated tool errors) via jsonl log with rolling 24h window. Threshold default=3, configurable. Instrumented runner.py and registry.py. 18 tests passing, all 381 suite ✓. Commit: 35b864cb.
